honoree.name: Sam J. Ervin
honoree.summary: Samuel James Ervin III was an American judge who served as a United States Circuit Judge of the United States Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit. He was born on October 13, 1926, and died on July 25, 2022. Ervin was nominated to the federal bench by President Richard Nixon and served from 1971 to 1999. He was the grandson of Senator Sam Ervin, who was famous for his role in the Watergate hearings.
honoree.education: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, Wake Forest University School of Law

honoree.name: James E. Holshouser
honoree.summary: James Holshouser was an American politician who served as the 66th Governor of North Carolina from 1973 to 1977. Born on October 6, 1934, in Boone, North Carolina, he was the first Republican governor of the state in the 20th century. He previously served in the North Carolina House of Representatives and later held a position on the North Carolina Board of Governors. He died on June 17, 2013, in Pinehurst, North Carolina.
honoree.education: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, Juris Doctor

honoree.name: John Penn
honoree.summary: John Penn was a North Carolina politician who served as a delegate to the Continental Congress. He was born on May 17, 1741, in Caroline County, Virginia. Penn represented North Carolina in the Continental Congress and signed the Articles of Confederation. He died on September 14, 1788, in Williamsburg, Virginia.
